title: Senior Housing Information and Referral * African American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Senior Housing Information and Referral * African American Community 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects older adults with services that allow them to remain safely in their homes and communities (elder law, home maintenance and minor repair services, housekeeping assistance, Medicare counseling, housing information and referral, advocacy, and senior ride program). Services include Oregon Project Independence Services (OPI) and community resources. Additionally offers short-term case management (Options Counseling) and chronic disease management. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 60 and older with a focus on the African-American community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE
